text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland Richard Payne and Matt Norris Third Sunday of Lent March 8 2026 This weekend March 7th 8th the Church celebrates the Third Sunday of Lent We enter now into one of the most ancient practices of the Church The Scrutinies from the Latin scrutinia or examinations Dating back to at least the 3rd Century those preparing for baptism at Easter undergo a series of rites during Lent that examine their readiness and includes prayers of exorcism and purification These practices were testified to by many of the early Church Fathers such as both St Ambrose of Milan and St Augustine of Hippo of the 4th Century in their Lenten sermons in preparation for the Easter Vigil By the 7th Century ritual books codified the sequence of these rites in great detail In addition to being prayed for those preparing for the Sacraments also received both the Creed and the Our Father from the local congregation As we begin the start of the Scrutinies this Sunday our Lectionary turns to the Gospel of St John for the remainder of Lent We are treated to the amazing trifecta of John 4 The Samaritan Woman John 9 The Man Born Blind and John 11 The Rich Man and Lazarus These are three of the most powerful chapters in one of the most spiritual books in the Bible as St John often represented by the soaring eagle takes us to the spiritual heights The Church turns to these texts from St John at this point because they are in so many ways mystagogical taking us deeper into the mysteries of the Church that is the Sacraments If one is not initiated into the Sacraments St John remains in many respects a book very difficult to comprehend For our Elect and Candidates for Full Communion preparing for initiation into the Sacraments these texts serve as a final sacramental catechesis Our Baptismal catechesis starts this weekend with the readings culminating in the Gospel of the Samaritan Woman The constant theme of living water is meant to be seen as the water of Baptism quenching the thirst of the soul and sharing eternal life For those of us who have already passed through the saving waters of Baptism may these readings remind us that the grace of Baptism isn't a thing of our past but is living and flowing within us even now The Light Is On For You This week our parish will join parishes throughout our diocese for the annual The Light Is On For You Campaign On Tuesday March 10th every parish in our diocese will be offering Confessions from 600 8:00PM Reminder Our weekly Confession times are Tuesdays from 830 9:00AM Wednesdays 6 6:45PM and Saturdays 830 9:00AM and 4 4:45PM Parish Garden Beds With the warm weather starting it is a good reminder that we will have garden beds available for use behind the Church If you would like to reserve a bed or a part of bed for use please contact the Parish Office (260)824-1380 for more information Church Renovation Updates Over the past two weeks Dancer Flooring has been prepping the poured terrazzo for completion With the arrival of our floor medallions this week they are now able to begin assembling the individual medallions and pour the remaining floor starting this week As of our last progress meeting Dancer is hoping to complete the nave flooring by the end of the month Once the nave flooring is completed we will be able to begin reinstalling the pews As this timeline will bring us right into Holy Week we are currently planning to hold our Triduum liturgies in the Parish Social Hall Given the detailed nature of these liturgies it would be very challenging to maneuver both Holy Week and moving back into the main Church at the same time As work continues we will revisit our timeline and share more information Saturday March 7 400 4:45pm Confession 4:35pm Rosary 5:00pm Our Parish Family Sunday March 8 7:10am Rosary 7:30am Living
